label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out if following these instructions brings us back to the starting point. I need to visualize the path step by step. First, the instructions are: 1. Turn right. 2. Take 9 steps. 3. Take 10 steps. 4. Turn left. 5. Take 10 steps. 6. Turn right. 7. Turn around. 8. Take 4 steps. Let me break it down step by step, keeping track of direction and movement. Assuming the starting direction is North (for simplicity, even though it doesn't matter as long as we are consistent): - Start facing North. 1. Turn right: Now facing East. 2. Take 9 steps: Moving East for 9 units. 3. Take 10 steps: Continuing East (since direction hasn't changed), total of 19 steps East. 4. Turn left: Now facing North. 5. Take 10 steps: Moving North for 10 units. 6. Turn right: Now facing East. 7. Turn around: Now facing West. 8. Take 4 steps: Moving West for 4 units. Now let's track the position: - East-West position: 19 steps East - 4 steps West = 15 steps East - North-South position: 10 steps North So the final position is 15 steps East and 10 steps North from the starting point. This is not the same as the starting point, so the answer would be No. Wait, let me double check my interpretation of the instructions: 1. Turn right. 2. Take 9 steps. 3. Take 10 steps. Is this 9 steps in one direction, then another 10 steps in the same direction (total 19 steps), or is there a direction change between steps 2 and 3? The instructions just say "Take 9 steps. Take 10 steps." with no mention of turning between them, so I think it's 19 steps in the same direction (East). 4. Turn left. 5. Take 10 steps. Now facing North and taking 10 steps North. 6. Turn right. 7. Turn around. This means turning right (now facing East), then turning around (now facing West). 8. Take 4 steps. So we are moving West for 4 steps. So final position is 19 - 4 = 15 steps East and 10 steps North from starting point. Not back to start, so answer is No. Actually wait, let me double check the direction changes again: Starting facing North. Turn right → East Take 9 steps. Take 10 steps. (Still East, total 19 steps East) Turn left → North Take 10 steps. (Still North, now 10 steps North) Turn right → East Turn around → West
